paper_title: Hitting all maximum stable sets in $P_5$-free graphs
summary: The conjecture that $P_t$-free graphs are $\eta$-bounded for every $t \geq 6$ remains open as of May 2026. The source paper establishes $\eta$-boundedness for $P_5$-free graphs (Theorem 1.12) and also resolves the $\eta$-boundedness for $H$-free graphs when $H$ is a proper induced subgraph of $P_5$. A separate 2025 result (Electronic Journal of Combinatorics) resolves a different conjecture from the same paper—bounding $h(G)$ for graphs without large induced matchings—but does not address $P_t$-free graphs for $t \geq 6$. No paper resolving or disproving Conjecture 1.13 was found in a wide web search.

internal_refs_verified.note: This paper (Chudnovsky, Fischer, Hajebi, Spirkl, Walczak, 2025) constructs a simple layered-wheel-like graph counterexample to Trotignon's conjecture about outerstring induced subgraphs and treewidth. It does not address $\eta$-boundedness or $P_t$-free graphs. The high fuzz score (81) is a false positive due to shared authors; the paper's subject is entirely unrelated to Conjecture 1.13.

search_queries: eta-boundedness Pt-free graphs stable sets hitting conjecture 2024 2025
"eta-bounded" "P_6-free" OR "P6-free" OR "Pt-free" hitting maximum stable independent sets 2024 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up paper resolving Conjecture 1.13 was found. The conjecture is recent (2024) and widely searched; open with high confidence. The related paper arXiv:2402.11222 and the Electronic Journal of Combinatorics paper 'Piercing Independent Sets in Graphs without Large Induced Matching' (v32i1p10, 2025) resolve a different conjecture from the same source paper (about graphs with bounded induced matching number), not Conjecture 1.13.
